title: Isla's New Friend
synopsis: Isla learns about making new friends and including others when she meets a shy boy at the playground. With her family's guidance, she discovers that friendship means being kind, patient, and welcoming to everyone.

pages.text: Isla loved going to the park with her mom and dad. She had long wavy red hair, very fair freckled skin, and bright green eyes that sparkled when she was happy. And today, she was very happy! The sun was shining, and the playground was full of children laughing and playing.

illustration.description: A sunny playground scene with Isla, who has long wavy red hair, very fair freckled skin, bright green eyes, walking with her parents toward colorful playground equipment with swings and slides. Diverse children are playing in the background.
illustration.alt: Isla with her parents approaching a bright, colorful playground

pages.text: "Can I go on the swings first?" Isla asked her mom. "Of course," Mom said with a smile. "Dad and I will be right here on the bench if you need us." Isla ran toward the swings, her red hair bouncing behind her. She loved how the swings made her feel like she was flying!

illustration.description: Isla with long wavy red hair, very fair freckled skin, bright green eyes running toward bright red and blue swings while her parents sit on a green park bench. Other diverse children are playing on slides and climbing equipment in primary colors.
illustration.alt: Isla running to the swings while her parents watch from a bench

pages.text: As Isla swung high in the air, she noticed a boy sitting alone under a big oak tree. He had dark hair and was wearing a blue shirt. He was watching all the other children play, but he wasn't playing with anyone. Isla wondered why he looked so sad.

illustration.description: Isla with long wavy red hair, very fair freckled skin, bright green eyes on a red swing looking toward a lonely boy with dark hair in a blue shirt sitting under a large green oak tree. The playground has bright primary colors with other diverse children playing happily.
illustration.alt: Isla swinging while looking at a lonely boy sitting under a tree

pages.text: After swinging for a while, Isla jumped down and walked over to where her parents were sitting. "Mom, Dad," she said, pointing to the boy under the tree. "That boy looks lonely. Why isn't he playing with the other kids?" Her mom looked over at the boy thoughtfully.

illustration.description: Isla with long wavy red hair, very fair freckled skin, bright green eyes talking to her parents on a green park bench, pointing toward the boy under the tree. The scene is bright and sunny with primary colored playground equipment and diverse children playing in the background.
illustration.alt: Isla talking to her parents about the lonely boy

pages.text: "Sometimes people feel shy when they're in a new place," Dad explained. "Maybe he's new to this park and doesn't know anyone yet. What do you think you could do to help?" Isla thought for a moment. She remembered how nervous she felt on her first day at a new playground last year.

illustration.description: Isla with long wavy red hair, very fair freckled skin, bright green eyes sitting between her parents on the bench, looking thoughtful. The playground scene shows bright primary colors with swings, slides, and diverse children playing. The lonely boy is still visible under the tree.
illustration.alt: Isla thinking while sitting with her parents

pages.text: "I could ask him if he wants to play!" Isla said excitedly. "That's a wonderful idea," Mom said. "But remember, if he says no, that's okay too. Some people need more time to feel comfortable." Isla nodded and walked slowly toward the boy under the tree.

illustration.description: Isla with long wavy red hair, very fair freckled skin, bright green eyes walking confidently toward the boy under the tree while her parents watch supportively from the bench. The playground has bright primary colors with other diverse children playing on swings and slides in the background.
illustration.alt: Isla walking toward the shy boy with her parents watching

pages.text: "Hi," Isla said gently when she reached the tree. "I'm Isla. I saw you sitting here, and I wondered if you'd like to play with me." The boy looked up with surprised brown eyes. "I'm Marco," he said quietly. "I just moved here last week. I don't know anyone yet."

illustration.description: Isla with long wavy red hair, very fair freckled skin, bright green eyes standing next to Marco, a boy with dark hair and brown eyes in a blue shirt, under a large green oak tree. The bright playground with primary colored equipment and diverse children is visible in the background.
illustration.alt: Isla introducing herself to Marco under the tree

pages.text: "Well, now you know me!" Isla said with a big smile. "Would you like to try the swings? They're my favorite!" Marco's face brightened a little. "I like swings too," he said, standing up slowly. "But I'm not very good at making friends." "Don't worry," Isla said. "I'll help you!"

illustration.description: Isla with long wavy red hair, very fair freckled skin, bright green eyes extending her hand to Marco who is standing up from under the tree, both smiling. The sunny playground with bright red and blue swings, yellow slides, and diverse children playing creates a joyful, inclusive scene.
illustration.alt: Isla helping Marco stand up, both beginning to smile

pages.text: Isla and Marco walked to the swings together. "See that girl on the slide with the yellow dress? That's Sarah. And the boy on the monkey bars is Ahmed. Everyone here is really nice," Isla explained. Marco listened carefully as they sat on the swings side by side.

illustration.description: Isla with long wavy red hair, very fair freckled skin, bright green eyes and Marco on bright red swings side by side, with Isla pointing to diverse children playing on yellow slides and blue monkey bars. The playground is full of primary colors and joyful energy.
illustration.alt: Isla and Marco swinging together while Isla introduces other children

pages.text: As they swung back and forth, Marco started to smile more. "This is fun!" he said. "I was worried that no one would want to play with me." "Why would you think that?" Isla asked. "You're really nice!" Marco blushed. "Thank you for being my friend, Isla."

illustration.description: Isla with long wavy red hair, very fair freckled skin, bright green eyes and Marco swinging high together, both laughing and smiling. The playground scene is bright and energetic with primary colors, showing diverse children playing happily on slides and climbing equipment.
illustration.alt: Isla and Marco swinging together, both happy and laughing

pages.text: Soon, Sarah came over from the slide. "Hi Isla! Who's your new friend?" she asked with a friendly smile. "This is Marco," Isla said proudly. "He just moved here!" Sarah waved at Marco. "Do you want to play tag with us?" Marco looked at Isla, who nodded encouragingly.

illustration.description: Isla with long wavy red hair, very fair freckled skin, bright green eyes, Marco, and Sarah (a diverse girl in a yellow dress) standing together near the bright red swings. The playground has primary colors with other diverse children visible playing on slides and climbing equipment in the background.
illustration.alt: Isla introducing Marco to Sarah near the swings

pages.text: "That sounds great!" Marco said, his shyness melting away. Soon Ahmed joined them too, and all four children were running around the playground, laughing and playing tag. Isla's parents watched from the bench, smiling at how kind their daughter was being to her new friend.

illustration.description: Isla with long wavy red hair, very fair freckled skin, bright green eyes running and playing tag with Marco, Sarah, and Ahmed (a diverse group of children) around bright playground equipment with primary colored swings, slides, and climbing structures. Her parents watch happily from a green bench.
illustration.alt: Four children playing tag together on the colorful playground

pages.text: When it was time to go home, Marco's mom came to pick him up. "Thank you for playing with Marco today," she told Isla. "He's been worried about making friends in our new neighborhood." Isla beamed. "Can Marco come back tomorrow?" she asked. "I'd love that," Marco said happily. Isla had made a wonderful new friend, and she couldn't wait to play together again.

illustration.description: Isla with long wavy red hair, very fair freckled skin, bright green eyes saying goodbye to Marco while both sets of parents talk nearby. The sunny playground with bright primary colors, swings, slides, and diverse children creates a warm, inclusive farewell scene.
illustration.alt: Isla and Marco saying goodbye with their parents at the playground

metadata.illustrationStyle: bright sunny playground, diverse kids playing, primary colors, white space, joyful energetic, inclusive, park swings slides, laughter, friendship activities

questions.question: Why was Marco sitting alone under the tree?
choices: He was new and didn't know anyone
He was waiting for his mom
questions.explanation: Marco was sitting alone because he had just moved to the neighborhood and didn't know anyone yet.

questions.question: What did Isla's parents teach her about helping shy people?
questions.explanation: Isla's parents taught her to be patient and understanding, and that it's okay if someone needs time to feel comfortable.

questions.question: What was Isla's favorite playground equipment?
questions.explanation: Isla loved the swings best because they made her feel like she was flying.

questions.question: How did Marco feel by the end of the story?
choices: Happy and excited to play again
questions.explanation: By the end, Marco was happy and excited to come back and play with his new friends the next day.

questions.question: What game did all the children play together?
questions.explanation: Sarah suggested playing tag, and all four children - Isla, Marco, Sarah, and Ahmed - played tag together.
